name: Sodium Percarbonate (Oxygen Bleach)
description: A granular adduct of sodium carbonate and hydrogen peroxide that releases active oxygen (H₂O₂) when dissolved in water, providing color-safe bleaching, stain removal, and sanitization without chlorine.
solubility: 140 g/L in water at 20°C; releases H₂O₂ and Na₂CO₃ upon dissolution
chemistry: Releases H₂O₂ in alkaline solution (pH ~10.5). Perhydroxyl anion (HOO⁻) is the active oxidant — far more potent than molecular H₂O₂. 13.5% active oxygen content. Requires warm/hot water for full activation.
warnings: Oxidizer — store away from flammables. Do NOT mix with chlorine bleach, vinegar, or ammonia. Not safe on silk, wool, or leather. Skin/eye irritant in concentrated solutions.
shelfLife: 1–2 years sealed and dry; degrades with moisture exposure
